fields.name: Histosol
fields.description: Soils with thick organic layers, typically found in wetlands and peat bogs.

fields.name: Anthrosol
fields.description: Soils with strong human influence through long-term cultivation or waste disposal.

fields.name: Technosol
fields.description: Soils containing significant amounts of technical artifacts or sealed by technical hard rock.

fields.name: Cryosol
fields.description: Soils with permafrost within 100 cm of the surface, found in polar and high mountain regions.

fields.name: Leptosol
fields.description: Very shallow soils over hard rock or highly calcareous material, with limited rooting depth.

fields.name: Solonetz
fields.description: Soils with high sodium content causing structural problems and limiting root penetration.

fields.name: Vertisol
fields.description: Clay-rich soils that shrink and swell significantly with moisture changes, creating cracks.

fields.name: Solonchak
fields.description: Salt-affected soils with high salinity that limits plant growth and root development.

fields.name: Gleysol
fields.description: Soils with groundwater influence showing redoximorphic features from alternating wet-dry conditions.

fields.name: Andosol
fields.description: Soils formed from volcanic ash with unique properties due to allophane and imogolite minerals.

fields.name: Podzol
fields.description: Acidic soils with distinct eluvial and illuvial horizons formed by podzolization process.

fields.name: Plinthosol
fields.description: Soils with plinthite that hardens irreversibly upon repeated wetting and drying.

fields.name: Planosol
fields.description: Soils with an abrupt textural change and temporary water stagnation.

fields.name: Stagnosol
fields.description: Soils with seasonal water stagnation in the upper part of the profile.

fields.name: Nitisol
fields.description: Deep, well-drained tropical soils with shiny ped faces and good structural stability.

fields.name: Ferralsol
fields.description: Highly weathered tropical soils dominated by iron and aluminum oxides with low fertility.

fields.name: Chernozem
fields.description: Dark, fertile grassland soils with thick mollic horizon rich in organic matter.

fields.name: Kastanozem
fields.description: Brown grassland soils with mollic horizon and secondary carbonates, drier than Chernozems.

fields.name: Phaeozem
fields.description: Dark soils with mollic horizon formed under forest or mixed vegetation.

fields.name: Umbrisol
fields.description: Acidic soils with thick, dark umbric horizon rich in organic matter.

fields.name: Durisol
fields.description: Soils with cemented horizon (duripan) formed by silica accumulation.

fields.name: Gypsisol
fields.description: Soils with substantial accumulation of secondary gypsum in the profile.

fields.name: Calcisol
fields.description: Soils with substantial accumulation of secondary carbonates in the profile.

fields.name: Retisol
fields.description: Soils with clay illuviation and interfingering of albic into argic horizon.

fields.name: Acrisol
fields.description: Acidic soils with low base saturation and clay accumulation in the subsoil.

fields.name: Lixisol
fields.description: Soils with clay accumulation and higher base saturation than Acrisols.

fields.name: Alisol
fields.description: Acidic soils with high aluminum saturation and clay accumulation.

fields.name: Luvisol
fields.description: Soils with clay illuviation and high base saturation, common in temperate regions.

fields.name: Cambisol
fields.description: Moderately developed soils with cambic horizon showing some alteration but little differentiation.

fields.name: Fluvisol
fields.description: Young soils formed from recent alluvial deposits with little profile development.

fields.name: Arenosol
fields.description: Sandy soils with weak profile development due to coarse texture and low clay content.

fields.name: Regosol
fields.description: Weakly developed soils with little or no profile differentiation, often on steep slopes.
